---
title: "Ownership Identification Inc.: Data manipulation and validation"
author: "Andre Bastos"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Ownership Identification Inc.: Data manipulation and validation

This is an R Markdown document. The code delivers a complete R workflow that transforms the raw spreadsheet data (.xlsx input) into a tidy, analysis‑ready format by removing extracting sheet‑level aggregation rows, systematically checking that the sum of monthly values matches the internally reported totals, and converting month labels into proper date objects suitable for time‑series analysis. In addition to producing a clean long‑format dataset (.csv) that retains only monthly observations, it also generates a separate validation report that documents any discrepancies between calculated and reported totals, allowing users to audit data quality without contaminating the main analytical dataset.

The following are key steps:
1. Load necessary packages for data manipulation and Excel reading
2. Excel sheet processor (clean + validate)
3. Run across all sheets
4. Extract year + quarter
5. Convert months to proper Date
6. Write validation report
7. Write output

Contact [alstats\@gov.bc.ca](mailto:alstats@gov.bc.ca) for any questions.

```{r Set-up, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# sets the time-out option to 25 mins
options(timeout = 1500)
```

```{r library}
# 1. Set-up and load library

# Install and load pacman if not already installed
if (!require('pacman')) {
install.packages('pacman')
library(pacman)
}

# Load or install the required packages
p_load(pacman, readxl, tidyverse, lubridate, janitor, install = TRUE, update = FALSE)

```


```{r processor}
# 2. Reference metadata

# Explicitly define months, flow blocks, and valid animal classes to avoid relying on positional logic.

MONTH_MAP <- tibble(
  month_label = c("Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
                  "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec"),
  month = 1:12
)

REGIONS <- c(
  "Kootenay", "Kamloops", "North Okanagan", "South Okanagan",
  "Cariboo", "Cent Interior", "Peace", "Non prescribed",
  "TOTAL- ALL REGIONS"
)

ANIMALS <- c("Bull", "Cow", "Ylg Heifer", "Ylg Steer", "Calves", "Total")

FLOW_TYPES <- c(
  "Cattle Shipped South",
  "Cattle Shipped East",
  "Total Livestock Remaining in B.C."
)


```

```{r sheets}
# 3. Robust month‑header detection

# Core ingestion function (per sheet)

# This function:
# - detects regions
# - extracts animal rows
# - pivots months
# - converts all dates to proper Date
# - preserves totals separately

process_sheet <- function(file, sheet) {

  year <- as.integer(sheet)

  raw <- read_excel(
    file,
    sheet = sheet,
    col_names = FALSE
  ) %>%
    clean_names() %>%
    rename(label = x1)

  # normalize label text
  raw <- raw %>%
    mutate(
      label = str_squish(str_to_title(label)),
      row_id = row_number()
    )

  # container for results
  out <- vector("list", nrow(raw))
  k <- 1

  # parser state
  current_region <- NA_character_
  current_flow   <- NA_character_
  current_animal <- NA_character_

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(raw))) {

    lbl <- raw$label[i]

    # ---- REGION ----
    if (!is.na(lbl) && lbl %in% REGIONS) {
      current_region <- lbl
      current_flow   <- NA_character_
      current_animal <- NA_character_
      next
    }

    # ---- FLOW TYPE ----
    if (!is.na(lbl) && lbl %in% FLOW_TYPES) {
      current_flow   <- lbl
      current_animal <- NA_character_
      next
    }

    # ---- ANIMAL CLASS ----
    if (!is.na(lbl) && lbl %in% ANIMALS) {
      current_animal <- lbl
      next
    }

    # ---- MONTH ROW ----
    if (!is.na(lbl) && lbl %in% MONTH_MAP$month_label &&
        !is.na(current_region) &&
        !is.na(current_flow) &&
        !is.na(current_animal)) {

      # iterate across value columns
      for (col in names(raw)[-c(1:2)]) {

        val_raw <- raw[[col]][i]
        val     <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(val_raw))

        if (!is.na(val)) {

          month_num <- MONTH_MAP$month[
            MONTH_MAP$month_label == lbl
          ]

          out[[k]] <- tibble(
            year = year,
            region = current_region,
            flow_type = current_flow,
            animal_class = current_animal,
            month = make_date(year, month_num, 1),
            value = val,
            source_sheet = sheet
          )
          k <- k + 1
        }
      }
    }
  }

  bind_rows(out)
}


```

```{r combine}
# 4. Load and combine all years

file <- "C:/Users/ABASTOS/Government of BC/SICI Data Library Subsite - Unpublished Data/OII_Cattle_Shipments.xlsx"

sheets <- excel_sheets(file)

long_data <- map_dfr(
  sheets,
  ~ process_sheet(file, .x)
)


```

```{r dates}
# 6. Internal validation: Check that monthly sums match reported totals (separate output)

# 6.1 Missing values

validation_missing <- long_data %>%
  filter(is.na(value))

# 5.2 Negative counts (should never exist)

validation_negative <- long_data %>%
  filter(value < 0)


# 5.3 Re‑computed total check (animal totals)

validation_totals <- long_data %>%
  filter(animal_class != "Total") %>%
  group_by(year, region, month) %>%
  summarise(recomputed = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  left_join(
    long_data %>%
      filter(animal_class == "Total") %>%
      select(year, region, month, reported = value),
    by = c("year","region","month")
  ) %>%
  mutate(diff = recomputed - reported) %>%
  filter(!is.na(reported) & diff != 0)

```

```{r output}
# 6. Write output

# Main data (long format)
write_csv(long_data, "oii_cattle_shipments_long.csv")

# Validation outputs (separate, auditable)
write_csv(validation_missing,   "validation_missing_values.csv")
write_csv(validation_negative,  "validation_negative_values.csv")
write_csv(validation_totals,    "validation_total_mismatches.csv")

```
